Lessons Learned: Thin premium trade — $0.40 credit on 10 contracts. 2x stop triggered correctly at $0.80 but the thin premium meant the stop was too sensitive to gamma noise. SPX never breached the strike. Identified minimum credit floor as a candidate rule (~$0.60 for SPX).

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.95 (+3.10%). Elevated vol regime. Signal: BEARISH (bear 3.0/5). SPX futures 7410. Pre-CPI tension. Oil $98.88. Breadth divergence — 52% above 50-day MA despite ATH.

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.70 (+1.74%). Elevated vol. CPI hot: 3.8% YoY headline, 2.8% core (both above expectations). SPX sold off to 7338 then recovered to 7366. Energy-driven inflation bleeding into shelter/services.
Lessons Learned: Post-CPI bear call spread. Thesis correct — SPX closed at 7366, well below 7395 strike. Stopped out on gamma noise from thin $0.35 premium. 2x stop at $0.70 triggered by a 15-point SPX move that never threatened the strike. Oversized at 15 contracts to compensate for thin premium — compounded the problem.
Mistakes: NONE - thin premium and oversizing identified as learning points. Stop functioned correctly.
Tags: Bear Call Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Stop-out, Thin Premium, Oversized

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX 18.70 (+1.74%). Elevated vol. CPI hot: 3.8% YoY headline, 2.8% core (both above expectations). SPX sold off to 7338 then recovered to 7366. Energy-driven inflation bleeding into shelter/services.
Lessons Learned: Reopened same position after stop-out, driven by frustration not thesis. Recognised the rule breach (same-market-after-loss gate, Rule 10) and closed quickly. Mistakes: NONE - rule breach. Rule 10 (same-market-after-loss gate) violated. Emotional reopen.

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX rising. PPI scorching hot: 1.4% MoM (vs 0.5% expected), 6.0% YoY. Core PPI 1.0% (vs 0.4% expected). Largest monthly PPI gain since March 2022. Rate hike odds climbed to 39%. SPX gapped down on open then recovered to new ATH 7438.
Lessons Learned: Unplanned entry — pre-market limit order from previous day's thesis filled at market open into hot PPI print. No plan for this trade existed. Cancelled stop under pressure when market chopped, then panic-closed at -$750 on a bounce. SPX recovered to 7438 — position would have expired worthless for full $700 profit. Cascade of errors from one operational mistake. Candidate Rule 19: unplanned entries get closed at market, no exceptions.
Mistakes: ERROR - Failed to cancel pre-market limit order. Unintended entry into PPI binary event. Cancelled stop under pressure. Panic close on bounce.
Tags: Bull Put Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Unplanned Entry, Stop Cancelled, Panic Close

Market Context: BEARISH — VIX rising. PPI scorching hot: 1.4% MoM (vs 0.5% expected), 6.0% YoY. Core PPI 1.0% (vs 0.4% expected). Largest monthly PPI gain since March 2022. Rate hike odds climbed to 39%. SPX gapped down on open then recovered to new ATH 7438.

Market Context: IC strategy. Gapped against position. Large loss. Account retired after this trade.
Lessons Learned: Gapped against position. Stopped out for -$750. KEY INSIGHT: Adding a second spread (call side) to create an IC on a directional thesis simply doubled the chance of being stopped out. Thesis was clear that market was rising — putting a bear call spread above on thin premium was pointless. It caused what would have been a profitable bull put trade to become the biggest loss. HARD RULE: No strategy alterations on the fly. Any change to strategy must go back through thorough backtesting and paper trading before live capital. No exceptions.
Mistakes: NONE - clean execution. Stop functioned correctly. Strategy under review.
Tags: Iron Condor, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, IC Strategy, Stop-out, Account Retired

Market Context: 0DTE BPS. SPX approached short strike mid-session. 2SD time-adjusted breach triggered exit discipline.
Lessons Learned: Closed on 2SD time-adjusted breach. SPX ~7560, ~24 pts from short strike, inside the band with session remaining. Endowment test: 'would I enter now? Hell no' — exit. Market bounced 2-3 pts inside band AFTER exit = outcome bias. Exit still correct on information available at the time. Felt live pressure, executed anyway — the freeze response did NOT fire. This is progress.
Tags: Bull Put Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, 2SD-exit, Endowment-test, No-freeze

Market Context: Flipped bullish as VIX fell -2.62% (to 15.64) and ES rallied. Falling-VIX to BPS rule firing via price action. Market context: House war-powers vote capping Iran escalation (NOT a peace deal — ceasefire being violated, Kuwait drone strike, Hezbollah rejection).
Lessons Learned: 72 pts OTM (~4 SD). Short delta and vega both favourable. Closed early at 86% of credit rather than letting expire — deliberate, to right-size the oversized tail risk. Banked $550 instead of carrying ~$19.3k of width into the live Iran tail. FLAG: This was a WIN on a RULE BREAK. Guard against outcome bias letting a profitable oversize reinforce oversizing behaviour.
Mistakes: ERROR - OVERSIZED. 10 lots = $20k width, ~$19.3k max loss = ~38% of net liq, ~2x the 20% high-conviction ceiling. Early close was correct risk management but the entry size was a rule breach.
Tags: Bull Put Spread, SPX, 0DTE, Live Account, Oversized, Rule-breach, Early-close
